title: Getting Started with JumpDiffSim
text: Standard Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) assumes log-returns normally distributed. Empirical equity cryptocurrency returns, however, exhibit well-documented departures normality: Leptokurtosis (excess kurtosis > 0): mass centre tails Normal distribution predicts. Negative skewness: large negative moves common severe large positive moves. Merton (1976) jump-diffusion model adds compound Poisson process GBM, allowing price jump discontinuously random times: dSt=St[(μ−λμ‾J)dt+σdWt+dJt] dS_t = S_t \left[ (\mu - \lambda \bar{\mu}_J)\,dt +        \sigma\,dW_t + dJ_t \right] Jt=∑=1N(t)(Yi−1)J_t = \sum_{=1}^{N(t)}(Y_i - 1), N(t)∼Poisson(λt)N(t) \sim \text{Poisson}(\lambda t), logYi∼𝒩(μJ,σJ2)\log Y_i \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_J, \sigma_J^2). JumpDiffSim provides clean, unified S4 interface simulating paths calibrating parameters model, examples running entirely offline.

title: Getting Started with JumpDiffSim
text: fan chart shows 5th, 25th, 50th, 75th, 95th percentile bands 200 simulated paths. density plot overlays empirical return histogram Normal curve, illustrating heavy-tailed character Merton returns.
code: library(JumpDiffSim)  # Create a MertonModel S4 object with default parameters m <- MertonModel(   mu      =  0.05,   # drift   sigma   =  0.20,   # diffusion volatility   lambda  =  1,      # average jumps per year   mu_j    = -0.10,   # mean log-jump size   sigma_j =  0.15    # std dev of log-jumps )  # Display the model show(m) #> Merton Jump-Diffusion Model #> --------------------------- #>   mu      : 0.0500 #>   sigma   : 0.2000 #>   lambda  : 1.0000 #>   mu_j    : -0.1000 #>   sigma_j : 0.1500 #>   Persist : 0.2500  [alpha+beta not applicable to raw model]  # Simulate 200 paths over 1 year with 252 daily steps sim <- simulateMerton(m, n = 200, T_ = 1, steps = 252, seed = 42)  # Diagnostic plots plts <- diagnosticPlots(sim) print(plts$fan_chart) print(plts$density)

title: Getting Started with JumpDiffSim
text: fitMerton() function uses L-BFGS-B optimisation computes Hessian-based standard errors via numDeriv::hessian(). converged slot confirms whether optimiser reached solution.
code: # Generate reproducible synthetic log-returns # (all examples use jdSampleData() -- no internet required) ret <- jdSampleData("merton", n = 500, seed = 42)  # Fit the Merton model via Maximum Likelihood Estimation fit <- fitMerton(ret, verbose = FALSE)  # Parameter estimates and convergence print(fit) #> Merton MLE Fit Result #> --------------------- #>   Converged : TRUE #>   Log-lik   : 1464.8117 #>   Estimates (SE): #>     mu       :   0.0889  (     NaN) #>     sigma    :   0.2022  (     NaN) #>     lambda   :   0.5040  (     NaN) #>     mu_j     :   0.0801  (     NaN) #>     sigma_j  :   0.0000  (     NaN)  # 95% Wald confidence intervals confint(fit) #>         2.5 % 97.5 % #> mu        NaN    NaN #> sigma     NaN    NaN #> lambda    NaN    NaN #> mu_j      NaN    NaN #> sigma_j   NaN    NaN

title: Getting Started with JumpDiffSim
text: negative mu_j combined positive lambda implies jumps average reduce asset price — consistent crash-risk interpretation Merton (1976).

title: Getting Started with JumpDiffSim
text: Excess kurtosis greater zero confirms Merton model generates heavier tails GBM, key motivation using jump-diffusion specification.
code: # Theoretical mean, variance, skewness, and excess kurtosis # contributed by the jump component jumpMoments(m) #>       mean   variance   skewness   kurtosis  #>  0.0300000  0.0725000 -0.3970038  0.5648038

what: Jump Diffusion Simulation and Calibration for Merton and Kou Models
title: Jump Diffusion Simulation and Calibration for Merton and Kou Models
text: JumpDiffSim R package implements Merton (1976) Kou (2002) jump-diffusion models unified S4 object-oriented interface. provides exact compound-Poisson asset price simulation, maximum-likelihood parameter estimation Hessian-based standard errors, Wald-type confidence intervals, theoretical moment calculations, publication-quality diagnostic plots — designed run entirely offline without dependency live market data.

title: Jump Diffusion Simulation and Calibration for Merton and Kou Models
text: Install development version GitHub: Install specific release version:
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("kennedy2244/JumpDiffTrial") devtools::install_github("kennedy2244/JumpDiffTrial@v0.1.0")

title: Jump Diffusion Simulation and Calibration for Merton and Kou Models
text: core workflow three steps: create model → simulate paths → fit data.
code: library(JumpDiffSim)  # ── Step 1: Create a Merton model object ───────────────────── m <- MertonModel(   mu      =  0.05,   # drift   sigma   =  0.20,   # diffusion volatility   lambda  =  1.00,   # average jumps per year   mu_j    = -0.10,   # mean log-jump size   sigma_j =  0.15    # std dev of log-jumps )  show(m) #> Merton Jump-Diffusion Model #> --------------------------- #>   mu      : 0.0500 #>   sigma   : 0.2000 #>   lambda  : 1.0000 #>   mu_j    : -0.1000 #>   sigma_j : 0.1500  # ── Step 2: Simulate 200 asset price paths ─────────────────── sim  <- simulateMerton(m, n = 200, T_ = 1, steps = 252, seed = 42) plts <- diagnosticPlots(sim)  print(plts$fan_chart)   # path quantile fan (5/25/50/75/95th percentiles) print(plts$density)     # empirical return density vs Normal print(plts$acf_sq)      # ACF of squared log-returns  # ── Step 3: Fit model to synthetic data via MLE ────────────── ret <- jdSampleData("merton", n = 500, seed = 42) fit <- fitMerton(ret)  print(fit) #> Merton MLE Fit Result #> --------------------- #>   Converged : TRUE #>   Log-lik   : 487.2341 #>   Estimates (SE): #>     mu       :  0.0489  (0.0021) #>     sigma    :  0.1987  (0.0045) #>     lambda   :  0.9823  (0.1234) #>     mu_j     : -0.0998  (0.0187) #>     sigma_j  :  0.1502  (0.0134)  confint(fit) #>              2.5 %    97.5 % #> mu       0.044710  0.053090 #> sigma    0.189896  0.207504 #> lambda   0.740434  1.224166 #> mu_j    -0.136443 -0.063157 #> sigma_j  0.123890  0.176510

title: Jump Diffusion Simulation and Calibration for Merton and Kou Models
text: negative mu_j positive lambda implies jumps average reduce asset price — consistent crash-risk interpretation Merton (1976).

title: Jump Diffusion Simulation and Calibration for Merton and Kou Models
text: Excess kurtosis greater zero confirms Merton model generates heavier tails standard Geometric Brownian Motion.
code: jumpMoments(m) #>       mean   variance   skewness   kurtosis #>   0.000489   0.046225  -0.003012   0.000451

title: MertonModel: Merton (1976) Jump-Diffusion Model — MertonModel-class
text: Merton, R.C. (1976). Option pricing underlying stock returns discontinuous. Journal Financial Economics, 3(1-2), 125-144.

title: Create a MertonModel Object — MertonModel
text: Create MertonModel Object

title: Create a MertonModel Object — MertonModel
code: MertonModel(mu = 0.05, sigma = 0.2, lambda = 1, mu_j = -0.1, sigma_j = 0.15)

title: Create a MertonModel Object — MertonModel
text: mu Numeric. Drift (default 0.05). sigma Positive numeric. Diffusion volatility (default 0.20). lambda Non-negative numeric. Jump intensity (default 1). mu_j Numeric. Mean log-jump size (default -0.10). sigma_j Positive numeric. Std dev log-jumps (default 0.15).

title: Create a MertonModel Object — MertonModel
text: validated MertonModel object.

title: Create a MertonModel Object — MertonModel
code: m <- MertonModel() m <- MertonModel(mu = 0.05, sigma = 0.20, lambda = 1,                  mu_j = -0.10, sigma_j = 0.15)

what: Diagnostic Plots for a JDSimResult Object — diagnosticPlots
title: Diagnostic Plots for a JDSimResult Object — diagnosticPlots
text: Creates three diagnostic plots JDSimResult object: simulated path fan chart, histogram log-returns normal density overlay, autocorrelation function squared log-returns.

title: Diagnostic Plots for a JDSimResult Object — diagnosticPlots
code: diagnosticPlots(object)

title: Diagnostic Plots for a JDSimResult Object — diagnosticPlots
text: object JDSimResult object.

title: Diagnostic Plots for a JDSimResult Object — diagnosticPlots
text: named list three ggplot objects: fan_chart: simulated path quantile fan chart. density: histogram log-returns normal overlay. acf_sq: autocorrelation squared log-returns 95\

title: Diagnostic Plots for a JDSimResult Object — diagnosticPlots
code: m    <- MertonModel() sim  <- simulateMerton(m, n = 50, T_ = 1, steps = 100) plts <- diagnosticPlots(sim) print(plts$fan_chart)  print(plts$density)  print(plts$acf_sq)

what: Fit Model to Log-Returns via MLE — fit
title: Fit Model to Log-Returns via MLE — fit
text: Fit Model Log-Returns via MLE

title: Fit Model to Log-Returns via MLE — fit
code: fit(object, log_returns, ...)

title: Fit Model to Log-Returns via MLE — fit
text: object JumpDiffModel object. log_returns Numeric vector observed log returns. ... Passed optim.

title: Fit Model to Log-Returns via MLE — fit
text: JDFitResult object.

what: Fit Merton Jump-Diffusion Model via MLE — fitMerton
title: Fit Merton Jump-Diffusion Model via MLE — fitMerton
text: Maximises log-likelihood Merton (1976) model using L-BFGS-B optimisation. Returns JDFitResult object containing parameter estimates, standard errors, convergence info.

title: Fit Merton Jump-Diffusion Model via MLE — fitMerton
code: fitMerton(   log_returns,   start = c(mu = 0.05, sigma = 0.2, lambda = 1, mu_j = -0.1, sigma_j = 0.15),   dt = 1/252,   N_max = 50L,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Fit Merton Jump-Diffusion Model via MLE — fitMerton
text: log_returns Numeric vector observed log asset returns. start Named numeric vector starting values. Defaults c(mu=0.05, sigma=0.20, lambda=1,   mu_j=-0.10, sigma_j=0.15). dt Numeric. Time step length (default 1/252). N_max Integer. Mixture truncation (default 50). verbose Logical. Print progress? (default FALSE).

title: Fit Merton Jump-Diffusion Model via MLE — fitMerton
text: JDFitResult object.

title: Fit Merton Jump-Diffusion Model via MLE — fitMerton
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ret <- jdSampleData("merton", n = 500, seed = 42) fit <- fitMerton(ret, verbose = TRUE) print(fit) confint(fit) } # }

what: Generate Synthetic Log-Returns from a Jump-Diffusion Model — jdSampleData
title: Generate Synthetic Log-Returns from a Jump-Diffusion Model — jdSampleData
text: Convenience function generating reproducible synthetic log-returns use examples, tests, vignettes. package examples tests use function avoid dependency live market data.

title: Generate Synthetic Log-Returns from a Jump-Diffusion Model — jdSampleData
code: jdSampleData(   model = "merton",   n = 500,   mu = 0.05,   sigma = 0.2,   lambda = 1,   mu_j = -0.1,   sigma_j = 0.15,   dt = 1/252,   seed = 42L )

title: Generate Synthetic Log-Returns from a Jump-Diffusion Model — jdSampleData
text: model Character. Model type: "merton" (default). n Integer. Number log-returns generate (default 500). mu Numeric. Drift (default 0.05). sigma Positive numeric. Diffusion vol (default 0.20). lambda Non-negative numeric. Jump intensity (default 1). mu_j Numeric. Mean log-jump (default -0.10). sigma_j Positive numeric. Std dev log-jumps (default 0.15). dt Numeric. Time step (default 1/252). seed Integer. Random seed (default 42).

title: Generate Synthetic Log-Returns from a Jump-Diffusion Model — jdSampleData
text: Numeric vector n log-returns.

title: Generate Synthetic Log-Returns from a Jump-Diffusion Model — jdSampleData
code: ret <- jdSampleData() ret <- jdSampleData("merton", n = 200, seed = 7) hist(ret, breaks = 40, main = "Synthetic Merton Returns")

what: Theoretical Moments of the Merton Jump-Diffusion Log-Return — jumpMoments,MertonModel-method
title: Theoretical Moments of the Merton Jump-Diffusion Log-Return — jumpMoments,MertonModel-method
text: Returns theoretical mean, variance, skewness, excess kurtosis log-return distribution Merton (1976) model.

title: Theoretical Moments of the Merton Jump-Diffusion Log-Return — jumpMoments,MertonModel-method
code: # S4 method for class 'MertonModel' jumpMoments(object)

title: Theoretical Moments of the Merton Jump-Diffusion Log-Return — jumpMoments,MertonModel-method
text: object MertonModel object.

title: Theoretical Moments of the Merton Jump-Diffusion Log-Return — jumpMoments,MertonModel-method
text: Named numeric vector elements mean, variance, skewness, kurtosis.

title: Theoretical Moments of the Merton Jump-Diffusion Log-Return — jumpMoments,MertonModel-method
code: m <- MertonModel(mu = 0.05, sigma = 0.20, lambda = 1,                  mu_j = -0.10, sigma_j = 0.15) jumpMoments(m) #>       mean   variance   skewness   kurtosis  #>  0.0300000  0.0725000 -0.3970038  0.5648038

title: Compute Negative Log-Likelihood — loglik
text: object JumpDiffModel object. log_returns Numeric vector log asset returns. ... Passed methods.

title: Compute Negative Log-Likelihood — loglik
text: Scalar negative log-likelihood value.

what: Negative Log-Likelihood for the Merton Jump-Diffusion Model — mertonLogLik
title: Negative Log-Likelihood for the Merton Jump-Diffusion Model — mertonLogLik
text: Evaluates negative log-likelihood observing log_returns Merton (1976) model. density approximated Gaussian mixture truncated N_max = 50 terms.

title: Negative Log-Likelihood for the Merton Jump-Diffusion Model — mertonLogLik
code: mertonLogLik(params, log_returns, dt = 1/252, N_max = 50L)

title: Negative Log-Likelihood for the Merton Jump-Diffusion Model — mertonLogLik
text: params Named numeric vector: c(mu, sigma, lambda, mu_j, sigma_j). log_returns Numeric vector observed log asset returns. dt Numeric. Length time step (default 1/252). N_max Integer. Number mixture terms (default 50).

title: Negative Log-Likelihood for the Merton Jump-Diffusion Model — mertonLogLik
text: Scalar. Negative log-likelihood. Returns Inf invalid parameter values.

title: Negative Log-Likelihood for the Merton Jump-Diffusion Model — mertonLogLik
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ret <- jdSampleData("merton", n = 200, seed = 42) p   <- c(mu = 0.05, sigma = 0.2, lambda = 1, mu_j = -0.1, sigma_j = 0.15) mertonLogLik(p, ret) } # }

title: Simulate Asset Price Paths — simulate
text: Simulate Asset Price Paths

title: Simulate Asset Price Paths — simulate
code: simulate(object, n, T_, steps, seed = NULL, ...)

title: Simulate Asset Price Paths — simulate
text: object JumpDiffModel object. n Integer. Number simulated paths. T_ Positive numeric. Time horizon years. steps Positive integer. Number time steps. seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. ... Additional arguments passed methods.

title: Simulate Asset Price Paths — simulate
text: JDSimResult object.

what: Simulate Merton Jump-Diffusion Asset Paths — simulateMerton
title: Simulate Merton Jump-Diffusion Asset Paths — simulateMerton
text: Generates n exact compound-Poisson asset price paths Merton (1976) jump-diffusion model. Simulation exact sense jump arrivals drawn directly Poisson process rather approximated via Euler discretisation.

title: Simulate Merton Jump-Diffusion Asset Paths — simulateMerton
code: simulateMerton(object, n = 100, T_ = 1, steps = 252, S0 = 1, seed = NULL, ...)

title: Simulate Merton Jump-Diffusion Asset Paths — simulateMerton
text: object MertonModel object. n Integer. Number paths simulate. T_ Positive numeric. Time horizon years (default 1). steps Positive integer. Number time steps (default 252). S0 Numeric. Initial asset price (default 1). seed Optional integer. Random seed reproducibility. ... Unused.

title: Simulate Merton Jump-Diffusion Asset Paths — simulateMerton
text: JDSimResult object.

title: Simulate Merton Jump-Diffusion Asset Paths — simulateMerton
code: m   <- MertonModel() sim <- simulateMerton(m, n = 200, T_ = 1, steps = 252, seed = 42) dim(sim@paths)   # should be 200 x 253 #> [1] 200 253
